test_refs: independent: python3 scripts/validate-graph-schema.py --graph <deficient sync payload> and gh-bridge.normalize_issue missing-key check
IN1.observed: Ran validate-graph-schema.py on a deficient sync payload: it failed closed (exit=1) enumerating every missing required key (graph_node_id via missing_id, github_publication.labels/milestone/project_aliases, plus dangling_dependency), so any pre-send payload with a required-key gap is rejected and only a 0-missing-key payload passes. gh-bridge.normalize_issue independently rejected a fetched issue missing id/url ('gh issue result missing fields: id, url'). pytest test_validate_graph_schema_and_main asserts the same violation-code set (missing_id, binding_collision, invalid_kind, dangling_dependency) and that a bad graph returns valid=false.

OUT1.observed: 2026-07-21 再取得 (r8)。commit 済み生成手順 (live_trial_shapes/shape_sync.py) の adapter fixture を使い、実 GitHub API へ一切接続しないオフライン再生で sync を 2 回実行。Live-trial verdict overall=PASS, tier=live, downgrade_reason=null, transcript_layer=jsonl, nudge=0/gate=0。1 回目で import/export が適用され、2 回目は imports changes=0 / exports changes=0、stable ID と snapshot は不変、gh-bridge.py の起動 0 件、C11 exit0。独立 evaluator が transcript で外部 API 非接触と冪等を再確認し PASS。

test_refs: independent: gh-bridge.normalize_issue updated_at exposure exercised directly
OUT2.observed: gh-bridge.normalize_issue surfaced updated_at ('2026-07-13T10:00:00Z') as the conflict hint that the id+updated_at tiebreak consumes. SKILL Protocol step 3 and github-lifecycle-contract.md define the rule: the newer updated_at wins, and simultaneous timestamps adopt the GitHub display value while leaving a local manual-confirmation flag. Independent review confirmed the plan layer keys the tiebreak off this normalized updated_at rather than physical overwrite.

test_refs: independent: live not-found deletion with Project linkage probe
OUT3.observed: Close and fixture/live not-found delete all preserve graph_node_id and artifact bytes while transitioning to closed/tombstoned. A deleted Issue with Project linkages planned zero Project mutations, so tombstone convergence is not blocked by a null content ID.

OUT4.observed: Independent audit confirmed sync --dry-run defaults to stdout only and changes neither graph, remote fixture, snapshot nor eval-log. Bridge mutation previews short-circuit before gh/bd writes and report write_count=0.

OUT5.observed: Independent review of R6-confirm (invariant purpose: read the manual-confirmation flag raised on id+updated_at simultaneous conflict as next-sync input, apply the user's confirmation result, then clear that node's flag) and SKILL Criteria acceptance OUT5. The resolve path is idempotent: once the confirmation result is applied on the follow-up sync the flag is dissolved, so a flagged node is cleared exactly by the confirmed re-sync and not left standing.

OUT6.observed: Pagination/dedup tests and independent probes confirmed one item per alias. A stale durable item ID yields manual conflict and no add; a null durable ID with one remote item adopts that ID through C02 and uses the same ID for field update, preventing duplicate or permanent retry.

OUT7.observed: Current 3-way tests confirm both-sides change becomes manual conflict and one-side change updates the snapshot. A defined but unset Project field is distinguished from a removed/renamed definition and remains writable; all mapping directions retain the local-only Status authority rule.

OUT8.observed: Rate-limit failure preserved local promotion and set only the alias pending_retry; after the cause disappeared a follow-up apply cleared last_error and returned the linkage to synced. Bridge probes covered single_select/text/number/date/iteration write payloads and retry classification.

OUT9.observed: Lifecycle tests confirm all waits for every linked PR, any accepts at least one eligible PR, and the writer never fabricates the requested unmerged PR as merged. Completion policy is recorded as linked_pr_merged_all|any; closed-unmerged and unsafe worktrees remain pending, with exact marker or Beads gh:pr gate required.
